result:
No leftover references. Here's what I found and fixed.

## What was wrong

This is a SaaS mid-pivot from an old product ("InboxZap," an email/inbox cleanup tool) to the current one ("Lumina," an AI meeting assistant) — and the migration was left half-done in ways that were actively suppressing organic traffic:

- **Every page's title/description/OG tags still said "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes"** (defined once in the root layout, never overridden), even though all visible content is about Lumina. Search results and social shares were selling a different product than the page delivered.
- **`/features` and `/pricing` 404'd.** Both are linked from primary nav, the homepage CTA, and were listed in `sitemap.xml` — so Google was crawling two "core pages" straight into dead ends.
- **The blog was completely broken.** `blog/page.tsx` fetched `/api/posts` client-side, but that API route doesn't exist in the codebase — it 404s. Crawlers and users only ever saw "Loading posts…". The 12 post URLs GSC shows historical impressions for (`/blog/post-1`...`post-12`, thousands of impressions each) don't correspond to any real route.
- The **`/faq` nav link redirected to `/#faq`**, but no element with `id="faq"` existed anywhere — a dead end.
- `sitemap.ts` had a literal `// TODO: include blog posts` and never did.
- The OG image (`og-inboxzap.png`) referenced in metadata didn't exist (404), and `metadataBase` was unset.

## What I did

- Rewrote metadata per-page (title template + unique title/description/canonical for home, features, pricing, blog index, and each post) instead of one static block covering every route.
- Built real `/features` and `/pricing` pages.
- Replaced the broken client-fetch blog with a proper server-rendered blog: `lib/posts.ts` as the data source, a real `/blog` index, and static `/blog/[slug]` pages (`generateStaticParams`, per-post metadata, `notFound()` for bad slugs).
- Added 6 genuine posts (not filler) targeting real search intent for the *current* product — meeting notes, action items, AI notetakers, meeting fatigue — rather than resurrecting old inbox-cleanup content for a product Lumina no longer sells.
- Fixed the sitemap to include features/pricing/all posts with real `lastModified` dates, and pointed the FAQ nav link straight at `/#faq`.
- Added a real FAQ section (with `id="faq"`) plus `FAQPage` and `BlogPosting`/`SoftwareApplication` JSON-LD structured data.
- Verified with `tsc --noEmit`, a full `next build` (all 14 routes compile, blog posts prerender as static HTML), and a `next start` smoke test of every route.

One thing I couldn't fix here: there's still no real OG image asset (no image tooling in this environment), so I removed the dangling reference rather than ship a broken one — worth having someone add a real 1200×630 social image.

The live-site URL you gave (`127.0.0.1:49812`) was unresponsive the whole time (confirmed via `lsof` — a process is bound to that port but never answers HTTP), so all verification above was done against a local `next build`/`next start` of this same source.
